name: United Citizens Bank & Trust Company
description_short: United Citizens Bank & Trust Company is a locally owned community bank serving six Kentucky communities since 1872, offering checking, savings, loans, and business credit cards with a focus on personalized customer service.
description_long:
United Citizens Bank & Trust Company traces its origins to 1872 with the founding of Bank of New Castle, making it one of Kentucky's oldest financial institutions. The bank has maintained its community-focused mission for over 150 years, operating as a locally owned institution rather than a national chain. Currently, the bank serves six Kentucky communities: Campbellsburg, New Castle, Eminence, Owenton (newly opened), Pendleton, Carrollton, and Buckner. Their CEO, Matthew Brent, emphasizes that the bank's enduring success stems from the strength of the communities it serves and its employees' commitment to the institution's values.

United Citizens Bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking services. Their product lineup includes checking and savings accounts, debit MasterCards, personal and home loans, and small business credit cards. The bank has modernized its digital offerings with a new United Citizens Online Banking platform and UCBMobile app available on both Android and Apple devices. They also provide financial tools and calculators to help customers with financial planning and decision-making.

The bank distinguishes itself through deep community involvement and sponsorships. Beyond traditional banking, UCB actively sponsors local events including the Watts Home Show, regional beef field days, community theater, county history center galas, youth livestock auctions, and 5K charity races. This engagement reflects their stated commitment to "invest in you, your family, and your hometown." Their messaging emphasizes being a "hometown bank rooted in customer service" with employees dedicated to helping customers "save, manage, invest, and grow."

While United Citizens Bank offers solid fundamentals for customers seeking a traditional community banking relationship, prospective customers should note that as a regional bank with only six physical locations in Kentucky, their service area is geographically limited. Their online and mobile platforms appear modernized, but specific details about account fees, interest rates, loan terms, and competitive advantages versus larger institutions are not detailed on their website. For consumers outside their service area or those seeking broad geographic flexibility, this bank may not be suitable. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with debit MasterCard access
Personal loans (home loans and other consumer lending)
Small business credit cards tailored for business needs
Online banking platform with secure digital access
UCBMobile app for iOS and Android devices
Financial tools and calculators for planning
Branch locations and customer service by phone (502-532-7392)
FAQ resources for account and lending questions
pros: 150+ year operating history dating back to 1872 provides institutional stability and community trust
Newly modernized online banking platform and UCBMobile app with user-friendly design for convenient digital access
Offers small business credit cards specifically designed for business customer needs
Active community sponsor with documented involvement in local events, charitable organizations, and youth programs
FDIC-insured institution (NMLSR #688969) providing deposit protection and regulatory oversight
Six convenient branch locations across central Kentucky with newly opened Owenton branch
Debit MasterCard option with simple, secure access for purchases and ATM withdrawals
cons: Limited geographic service area restricted to six Kentucky communities, making it inaccessible for out-of-state customers or those in rural areas outside their footprint
Website lacks specific information about account fees, minimum balances, interest rates, and APRs needed for product comparison
No mention of advanced digital banking features like remote deposit capture, bill pay functionality, or investment services that larger banks typically offer
Small regional bank may offer fewer credit card rewards programs or premium account benefits compared to national banking competitors
Limited information about loan products, terms, and rates makes it difficult to assess competitiveness of their mortgage and personal loan offerings
best_for: Kentucky residents in Campbellsburg, New Castle, Eminence, Owenton, Pendleton, Carrollton, or Buckner seeking personalized community banking
Small business owners in central Kentucky needing local business banking relationships and credit card solutions
Customers prioritizing deep community involvement and local sponsorship support from their financial institution
Long-term customers valuing stability and 150+ year institutional heritage over digital-first or national bank options
diagnosis: United Citizens Bank is best for central Kentucky customers in their six-county service area who value long-standing community relationships, stability, and local involvement over digital convenience or national reach. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—this bank only serves a specific region of Kentucky, making it unsuitable for customers outside these communities or those requiring multi-state banking flexibility.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
